verified_foundations: Operational certificate validation rejects future-issued, expired, inactive, stale-revocation, fixture-only and non-production-trust use.
The frozen invalidation event is strict, canonical-hashed, trusted producer-journal signed, per-target, strictly authority-lowering, action-complete and closed by reason/target/action policy.
Legacy autonomy lifecycle overlays use collision-resistant label/context/pipeline-fingerprint paths and an exact multi-person risk-bucket/context/fingerprint stratum.
Serious single- and multi-person failure tests prove serving/training eligibility removal for the affected legacy scope while an unrelated stratum remains usable.

focused_verification.command: python -m pytest -q tests/test_mask_bridge_contracts_v1.py -k 'invalidation or certificate_cannot_escape' tests/test_autonomy_operations.py tests/test_autonomy_multi_person_operations.py
Ruff plus invalidation schema JSON parsing

gap_probe.observed: Both records validate independently, but there is no input or decision that applies the current signed event/revocation head or live pipeline, ontology, policy and provider lifecycle to the immutable certificate.
gap_probe.required_behavior: At use time, recompute exact certificate scope/current dependencies, replay a trusted contiguous invalidation journal to an independently pinned head, and return active or a typed lowering decision without rewriting the certificate or unrelated scope.

missing_acceptance: One deterministic operational-certificate authority decision binding certificate hash, decision time, current revocation head and exact live pipeline fingerprint, ontology, policy, provider/model/workflow/runtime and provider-lifecycle identities.

Lossless signed-event application with exact target matching, effective-time behavior, idempotent duplicate handling, fork/delete/reorder rejection, historical reconstruction and concurrent read/write consistency.
Expiry and every drift/revocation cause lower future authority only for the exact affected scope, while immutable historical certificate bytes and unrelated targets remain unchanged.
Closed typed reasons and tests for certificate, pipeline, ontology, policy, provider lifecycle, signer/key and release/capability invalidation.

worker_state.request_scope_problem: The request permits mutation of frozen v1 mask_authority_invalidation_event schema and positive operational certificate fixture, and its scope packet is rooted under C:\Comfy_UI_Main rather than the canonical MaskFactory packet root.
worker_state.required_disposition: Do not duplicate, wake, mutate or adopt it. First terminalize/cancel it through the authorized control plane, then issue a new additive exact-task unit that leaves all 12 frozen v1 contracts and fixtures byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This revalidation supports honest partial progress only. It does not apply an invalidation to an operational certificate, complete MF-P6-08.07, adopt a worker result, or claim the autonomous certificate/revocation runtime is production-ready.
